metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Verified gate description: verify each named check against gerrit-verify.yaml and remove the env-registry drift gate from the list (it runs in branch CI test.yml only, not in the Verified Gerrit gate).
findings.finding: The plan states that the Verified gate (gerrit-verify.yaml) 'additionally runs the...env-registry drift gates,' but code inspection shows the env-var registry drift gate (gen_env_registry.py / docs/env-vars.md check) is present in test.yml (branch CI) and absent from gerrit-verify.yaml. The produced docs/review-policy.md would misstate what the authoritative Verified workflow actually checks, directly contradicting the plan's own instruction that the document be 'described accurately' via the linked workflow file.
findings.location: What section — Verified label description: 'the workflow additionally runs the rebar-ticket trailer check, module-size gate, prompt-index/env-registry drift gates...'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Verified description to: 'the workflow additionally runs the rebar-ticket trailer check, module-size gate, prompt-index drift gate, security-rules freshness, criteria-routing parity, public-types drift, `make config-check`, pip-audit, and unit + integration pytest tiers.' Remove 'env-registry drift gate' from the enumeration; optionally note that the env-var registry drift gate runs in branch CI (test.yml) but not in the Gerrit Verified gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC4's grep portability: replace `grep -c "llm-unavailable\|gate-disabled" CONTRIBUTING.md` with `grep -cE "llm-unavailable|gate-disabled" CONTRIBUTING.md` (ERE with `-E` works consistently across GNU and BSD grep) and assert the result equals 0.
findings.finding: The AC4 proving command `grep -c "llm-unavailable\|gate-disabled" CONTRIBUTING.md` uses BRE alternation (`\|`) which works in GNU grep but silently matches a literal `\|` on macOS BSD grep, making the zero-count assertion non-portable and potentially passing when it should fail (or failing to match at all on a contributor's Mac).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC grep (e.g. `grep -qi 'personally explain\|AI-assisted' docs/review-policy.md`) to cover the responsibility-clause deliverable so it cannot be omitted while all criteria still pass.
findings.finding: The 'responsibility clause' deliverable — 'the submitter must be able to personally explain their change, AI-assisted or not' — is described in the 'What' section but has no covering acceptance criterion that verifies it appears in the doc.
findings.location: What section (responsibility clause paragraph) / Acceptance Criteria list
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1 (or add a new AC bullet): `grep -qi "personally explain" docs/review-policy.md` and/or `grep -qi "AI-assisted" docs/review-policy.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Verified gate description: confirm that the env-registry drift gate (`gen_env_registry.py --check`) is absent from `gerrit-verify.yaml` and either add it to that workflow or remove 'env-registry drift' from the doc's Verified gate enumeration.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `gerrit-verify.yaml` runs 'prompt-index/env-registry drift gates' (plural, paired), but no env-registry drift gate exists in `gerrit-verify.yaml`. The `gen_env_registry.py --check` step appears only in `.github/workflows/test.yml` (line 255), not in `gerrit-verify.yaml`'s `build-and-test` job. The policy doc will therefore describe the Verified gate as including an env-registry drift check that does not actually run on Gerrit patchsets.
findings.location: What section: 'Verified described via the workflow' paragraph; also AC bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Remove 'env-registry drift' from the paired 'prompt-index/env-registry drift gates' phrase so it reads 'prompt-index drift gate'. If the intent is that both gates guard Verified, add a step to gerrit-verify.yaml that mirrors test.yml's `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py --check` step, then update the doc accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC4's conditional check as an explicitly gated sub-item (e.g. 'Only after fat-leaflike-canary merges: `grep -q "review-policy" docs/your-first-change.md`') so its precondition is unambiguous to both humans and automation.
findings.finding: AC4 embeds a conditional post-dependency check (`post-dependency, grep -q "review-policy" docs/your-first-change.md`) as a flat checklist item with no explicit guard, meaning automated or eager-reviewer checks will report a false failure before fat-leaflike-canary lands.
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC4 into two bullets: one for the CONTRIBUTING.md checks (unconditional) and one prefixed '(post fat-leaflike-canary)' for the your-first-change.md check, so the conditionality is syntactically obvious.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the Scope IN-list and enumerate an index entry for `docs/review-policy.md` as an edit target (consistent with how fat-leaflike-canary handles the same file for its tutorial).
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` is the docs index (confirmed in repo) and would naturally need an entry for the new `docs/review-policy.md` so contributors can discover it, but the plan's Scope and edit list do not enumerate `docs/README.md` as an edit target.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `docs/README.md` (new entry under the Contributor section); add a corresponding AC: `grep -q 'review-policy' docs/README.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a `depends_on` edge (and sequencing note) to the story that creates `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, confirm the file already exists, or add an AC grep (`test -f docs/plan-review-gate.md`) to catch the gap before merge.
findings.finding: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` is referenced as a live link target inside the new `docs/review-policy.md` ('One clarifying paragraph … linking docs/plan-review-gate.md') but the plan gives no sequencing note, no `depends_on` edge, and no existence guarantee for that file — unlike the carefully handled `docs/your-first-change.md` dependency. If the file is absent at merge time, the new policy doc ships a dangling reference.
findings.location: What section — 'One clarifying paragraph distinguishing THIS … linking docs/plan-review-gate.md'; Acceptance Criteria — no AC grep verifies this file exists
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sequencing note parallel to the one for `docs/your-first-change.md`: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md is created by story <name>, which this story depends_on (edge recorded in the store)' — or, if the file already exists, add `grep -q 'plan-review-gate' docs/review-policy.md` to the AC and note its pre-existence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either link the research artifact (search log, dated survey, ADR entry) that supports the 'first-of-kind in public OSS' blocking-LLM-vote claim, or soften the framing in the Why section to 'no widely-documented public precedent found as of [date]' so the claim is bounded and falsifiable.
findings.finding: The plan's core 'Why' rationale rests on an unverified novelty claim — 'research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote' — but names no source, links no artifact, and describes no search methodology. This claim is load-bearing: it is the stated justification for 'novelty raises the documentation bar,' which in turn motivates the policy doc's scope (dispute path, bypass, responsibility clause). If comparable prior art exists (e.g., an internal-OSS project or a newer policy doc post-dating the search), the framing and content guidance in the new document may need adjustment.
findings.location: Why section — 'research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote' with 'no widely-documented public precedent for a blocking LLM merge vote was found as of [month year]; see [link-to-search-artifact-or-ADR] for the survey scope' — or add a footnote/ADR entry that records the search queries and sources checked.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the What section how the `(merge-change, N integrated commit(s))` suffix variant is to be presented in the vote-semantics table — e.g. 'as a parenthetical note in the PASS and BLOCK–finding rows' or 'as a dedicated merge-change column' — so the executor has a determinate layout to implement.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the vote-semantics table covers both 'the distinct coverage-gap (merge-review) veto' AND 'the (merge-change, N integrated commit(s)) suffix variant' but never specifies how the merge-change suffix variant is to be presented in the table — as its own rows, as parenthetical sub-variants within existing rows, or as a footnote. Since the suffix is appended to ANY base reason (PASS, finding, or a coverage-gap sub-reason per adapter.py:70-73), the executor writing the doc faces an underdetermined layout decision with no guidance, risking either a confusing over-expanded table or an under-specified one that omits a tag contributors will see on merge changes.
findings.location: What section — Vote-semantics table description: 'the distinct coverage-gap (merge-review) veto (~line 160) and the (merge-change, N integrated commit(s)) suffix variant'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the table description: 'The merge-change suffix ((merge-change, N integrated commit(s))) is presented as a parenthetical note within the PASS and BLOCK—finding rows (not as separate rows), clarifying that the verdict semantics and productive response are identical for merge changes.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
